## Summary

Adds `docs/architecture/phase-v2-runtime-split-plan.md`, a staged runtime implementation plan for moving from the current mixed lifecycle to Phase Architecture v2.

## Scope

This is intentionally a planning/contract PR, not a broad runtime rewrite.

It defines the safe sequence:

1. Canon and naming freeze
2. Pass 3A gate-validity contract
3. Progress/state model support
4. Track C extraction
5. Review Gate hardening
6. Phase 2 guard
7. UI/log/progress label cleanup

## Key rules

- First runtime goal is correctness, not speed.
- Track B and Track C must be independently throttled.
- Pass 3A REDUCE waits for all MAP chunks.
- Review Gate readiness is derived, not hand-set.
- Failed Pass 3A blocks; degraded Pass 3A is allowed only with structured proof.
- No WAVE / Quality Gate naming collapse.

## Why

The repo appears to contain most of the machinery, but the orchestration boundary is muddy. This plan gives Codex/GitHub a safe incremental path instead of a risky all-at-once concurrency rewrite.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Runtime split sequence is documented.
- [ ] Non-goals are explicit.
- [ ] Suggested failure/status codes are listed.
- [ ] Test coverage targets are listed.
